message:
fix(tracker): ensure file renames are detected

- Use --no-renames with git status to force split D/A events
- Prevents 'R old -> new' lines from being ignored by snapshot parser
- Fixes issue where renamed files were tracked as deleted but not added
- Fixes bd-2xl3z

files.patch:
@@ -190,6 +190,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2wih","title":"Task: Add --stagger flag to spawn","description":"## Purpose
Add staggering option to spawn command for rate limit avoidance.

## Flag
ntm spawn myproject --cc=5 --stagger=smart|fixed|none

## Stagger Modes
- smart: Use learned optimal delays from RateLimitTracker
- fixed: Use fixed delay (--stagger-delay=15s)
- none: No staggering (default for backward compatibility)

## Implementation
func (s *SpawnCmd) spawnWithStagger(agents []AgentSpec) error {
    delay := s.rateLimitTracker.GetOptimalDelay(agents[0].Provider)
    
    for i, agent := range agents {
        if i > 0 && s.staggerMode \!= 'none' {
            time.Sleep(delay)
        }
        
        if err := s.spawnAgent(agent); err \!= nil {
            if isRateLimitError(err) {
                s.rateLimitTracker.RecordRateLimit(agent.Provider)
                delay = s.rateLimitTracker.GetOptimalDelay(agent.Provider)
                i-- // Retry
                continue
            }
            return err
        }
        s.rateLimitTracker.RecordSuccess(agent.Provider)
    }
    return nil
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Staggered spawning works
- Retries on rate limit
- Progress shown during spawn
- Default is backward compatible

## Technical Notes
Add flag to existing spawn command","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-2wkrv","title":"Implement timing constants exposure","description":"Implement timing constants exposure.

## Fields

- ctrl_c_gap_ms: 100 (gap between Ctrl-Cs for exit)
- post_exit_wait_ms: 3000 (wait for shell after exit)
- cc_init_wait_ms: 6000 (wait for claude code init)
- prompt_submit_delay_ms: 1000 (delay before prompt)

## Critical Insight

These timings were discovered through operational experience:
- 100ms Ctrl-C gap is REQUIRED (slower doesn't work)
- 6s is typical claude code startup time
- Adjustments may be needed per system

## Configuration Override

Allow config file override for system-specific tuning:
```yaml
timing:
  ctrl_c_gap_ms: 100
  post_exit_wait_ms: 3000
  cc_init_wait_ms: 6000
```

## Source Priority

1. Config file override (if present)
2. Environment variable override
3. Built-in defaults","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:35.914887875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:35.943546414Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wkrv","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:35.943514203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2wzs","title":"Add ntm send --priority-order - send to agents in order of bead priority (P0 first)","description":"## Overview
Add --priority-order flag to ntm send to deliver prompts to agents in order of bead priority (P0 first, P4 last).

## Problem
When assigning multiple beads, critical work should start first. Default round-robin ignores priority.

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --priority-order` sorts beads by priority before assignment.

## Implementation
```go
func sortByPriority(beads []Bead) []Bead {
    sorted := make([]Bead, len(beads))
    copy(sorted, beads)
    sort.Slice(sorted, func(i, j int) bool {
        return sorted[i].Priority < sorted[j].Priority // 0 = highest
    })
    return sorted
}
```

## Priority Order
- P0 (critical) → sent first
- P1 (high) → sent second
- P2 (medium) → sent third
- P3 (low) → sent fourth
- P4 (backlog) → sent last

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Send in priority order
ntm send myproject --priority-order

# Combine with assignment
ntm assign myproject --priority-order --auto

# Show what would be sent
ntm send myproject --priority-order --dry-run
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] P0 beads sent before P1, etc.
- [ ] Stable sort (same priority maintains order)
- [ ] Works with --batch and assign
- [ ] Logging shows priority order
- [ ] Compatible with --limit

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Priority sorting algorithm
- Unit test: Stable sort within same priority
- E2E test: Priority-ordered batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-308e","title":"Feature: Extend metrics export (JSON + Prometheus)","description":"## Overview
NTM already exposes analytics/metrics. This task extends the existing metrics export to support Prometheus‑friendly output and richer JSON, rather than introducing a parallel metrics system.

## Scope
- Reuse current `ntm analytics` / `--robot-metrics` data
- Add Prometheus text format export (if requested)
- Ensure JSON output includes labels (session, agent_type, model)

## Tests
- Unit: JSON + Prometheus formatting
- E2E: run metrics export and validate schema + sample values

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-METRICS] format=%s sessions=%d bytes=%d", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplication of metrics collection
- Prometheus output is opt‑in and documented
- Unit + E2E tests cover export formatting","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:35.438237930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:36:08.978895795Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"ntm-jv8p","title":"EPIC: Context Window Rotation","description":"Monitor context window usage and proactively rotate agents before exhaustion.

## Problem Statement
Claude Code agents have finite context windows. When an agent exhausts its context:
1. It may miss earlier instructions or become inconsistent
2. User must manually detect this and restart
3. Work in progress may be lost
4. No graceful handoff to fresh agent

The existing Health & Resilience EPIC (ntm-6d5p) handles crashes and hangs, but NOT context exhaustion - a different failure mode.

## CRITICAL DESIGN CONSIDERATION: Token Estimation Accuracy

**The Fundamental Challenge:**
We cannot accurately know an agent's true context window usage from outside because:
1. Claude Code manages its own conversation history internally
2. Claude Code may compact/summarize history automatically
3. Token counts from robot mode show throughput, not cumulative context
4. Different models handle context differently

**Proposed Solutions (implement multiple):**

### A. Robot Mode Context Reporting (Preferred)
If Claude Code exposes context usage in robot mode output:
\`\`\`json
{
  "type": "status",
  "context_used": 145000,
  "context_limit": 200000,
  "context_percent": 72.5
}
\`\`\`
This is the most accurate source. Check if available.

### B. Message Count Heuristic
Track message count as a proxy:
\`\`\`go
type ContextEstimate struct {
    MessageCount     int
    EstTokensPerMsg  int     // Configurable, default 1500
    EstimatedUsage   int64
    Confidence       float64 // Lower confidence than direct reporting
}
\`\`\`

### C. Session Duration + Activity Heuristic
Estimate based on time and activity:
- Long-running sessions with high output are likely near exhaustion
- Combine with message count for better estimate

### D. Conservative Thresholds
Given uncertainty, use conservative thresholds:
- Default warning at 60% estimated (might be 80% actual)
- Default rotate at 80% estimated (might be 95% actual)
- Make thresholds highly configurable

## Graceful Degradation Before Rotation

Before rotating, try these in order:
1. **Ask agent to compact**: "Please summarize our conversation so far"
2. **Trigger built-in summarization**: If Claude Code supports /compact
3. **Pre-rotation checkpoint**: Capture state BEFORE threshold
4. **Then rotate**: If above doesn't help

## Solution Overview
1. **Detection**: Multiple estimation strategies with confidence levels
2. **Graceful Degradation**: Compact before rotate
3. **Pre-rotation Checkpoint**: Checkpoint at warning threshold, not rotate threshold
4. **Thresholds**: Configurable warning/rotate thresholds (conservative defaults)
5. **Rotation Strategy**: Spawn fresh agent, transfer context summary, kill old agent
6. **Continuity**: New agent receives summary of work in progress
7. **User Confirmation**: Optional confirmation before auto-rotation

## Core Components

### 1. Context Estimation (Multi-Source)
\`\`\`go
type ContextEstimator struct {
    strategies []EstimationStrategy
}

type EstimationStrategy interface {
    Estimate(agentID string) (*ContextEstimate, error)
    Confidence() float64
}

// Implementations:
// - RobotModeStrategy (if available, highest confidence)
// - MessageCountStrategy (medium confidence)
// - DurationActivityStrategy (low confidence)
\`\`\`

### 2. Rotation Thresholds
\`\`\`toml
[context_rotation]
enabled = true
warning_threshold = 0.60    # Conservative: warn early
rotate_threshold = 0.80     # Conservative: rotate before problems
summary_tokens = 2000
min_session_age_sec = 300
require_confirmation = false  # Ask user before rotating
pre_rotation_checkpoint = true
try_compact_first = true
\`\`\`

### 3. Handoff Summary Generation (with Pre-Rotation Checkpoint)
When approaching warning threshold:
1. Create checkpoint (before problems start)
2. At rotate threshold, request summary
3. If summary fails, use checkpoint's context

### 4. Rotation Flow (Revised)
\`\`\`
1. Detect agent approaching warning_threshold
2. Create pre-rotation checkpoint
3. At rotate_threshold:
   a. If try_compact_first: Ask agent to compact
   b. Wait and re-check usage
   c. If still high: proceed with rotation
4. Mark agent as "rotating" (no new work)
5. Request handoff summary from agent
6. Spawn replacement in SAME PANE POSITION (not new pane)
7. Send handoff summary as initial context
8. Wait for new agent ready
9. Replace old agent pane (same ID: cc_1 stays cc_1)
10. Record rotation in history
\`\`\`

## Agent Identity on Rotation
The rotated agent keeps the same identity:
- cc_1 at 95% context → rotate → cc_1 at 0% context
- Dashboard shows: "cc_1 (rotated 2m ago)"
- Rotation history tracked for debugging

## Dashboard Integration
- Show context usage % on each pane card
- Color coding: green (<40%), yellow (40-60%), orange (60-80%), red (>80%)
- Rotation indicator with timestamp
- Compact button (manual trigger)

## Deliverables
1. Multi-source context estimation with confidence levels
2. Graceful degradation (compact before rotate)
3. Pre-rotation checkpointing integration
4. Configurable rotation thresholds (conservative defaults)
5. User confirmation option
6. Seamless agent rotation with identity preservation
7. Rotation history/audit log
8. Dashboard context usage display
9. Rotation events in alert system

## Why This Matters
- Prevents context exhaustion failures
- Maintains work continuity across rotations
- Enables truly long-running orchestrated sessions
- Complements health monitoring for complete reliability

## Dependencies
- Health & Resilience EPIC provides process restart infrastructure
- Checkpointing EPIC for pre-rotation checkpoints
- Uses existing robot mode token reporting (if available)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"Claude-Code-Explorer","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:11:43.916009152Z","updated_at":"2026-01-20T01:54:26.740831642Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
